description_long: This object consists of three elegant white flowers with trumpet-shaped blooms, each perched atop a long, slender green stem. The flowers resemble calla lilies, with smooth, gently curving petals that form a funnel around a central yellow spadix. The stems are straight and upright, with a subtle gradient from green to a slightly darker base. No leaves are visible, emphasizing the vertical, minimalist aesthetic of the arrangement. The petals have a soft, matte finish, and the overall structure is delicate and lightweight. This floral grouping would be commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or as decorative elements in public spaces.
description: A trio of white, trumpet-shaped flowers with long green stems and no visible leaves, resembling calla lilies. The arrangement is elegant and minimalistic, with smooth, gently curved petals.
description_view_0: Three white, trumpet-shaped flowers with yellow centers are visible, each on a long green stem, with the blooms facing slightly outward.
description_view_1: The flowers are seen from the side, showing the curvature of the petals and the alignment of the stems, with one bloom partially obscured.
description_view_2: The arrangement is viewed from the opposite side, highlighting the elongated stems and the open, flared shape of the petals.
description_view_3: The flowers are viewed from the back, with the stems and the undersides of the petals most prominent, and the blooms angled away.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are fused into a single, trumpet-shaped spathe that wraps around the central spadix, displaying a bilateral symmetry typical of calla lilies. The arrangement is smooth and continuous, with no distinct separation between individual petals.
